reforms.description: The Slovak Republic aims to enhance the educational system and improve the quality of education and training programs to address the lack of highly qualified human capital. This includes increasing resources for education, upgrading skills, and enhancing vocational training to boost employability and raise educational attainment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving education and training systems is expected to enhance the skills of the workforce, leading to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been successfully put into practice as part of the National Employment Plan and the National Programme for Upbringing and Education.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant structural change aimed at addressing critical barriers to economic progress through human capital development.
reforms.source_quote: The government submitted to Parliament a programme in 2001 – The National Programme for Upbringing and Education – setting out a strategy for improving the quality of the education and training systems.
